subject: Basement flooding after Tuesday's storm — 2218 Bohland Ave
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, after Tuesday night's storm we had about four inches of water in our finished basement. The floor drain backed up and pushed water in rather than draining it. This has happened twice in two springs now. My neighbor two doors down says the same thing. I'm not sure if the storm sewer on Bohland is undersized or just blocked, but something is wrong. We've lost carpet, a dehumidifier, and part of a drywall ceiling. Please, someone needs to look at this before the next big rain.

subject: Basement flooding after Tuesday's storm — 2218 Bohland Ave
body_clean: Ms. Szymanski, I got your message through the Mayor's office and sent a crew to the catch basin at Bohland and 22nd yesterday afternoon. They pulled a significant debris load — compacted leaves and sediment had cut inlet capacity by roughly half. Basin is cleared now. I also walked the block and the lateral to the trunk main on Washington looks intact, so the blockage was the likely culprit. If you get another backup after the next rain event let me know immediately so we can camera the line. I'm sorry about your basement.

subject: Standing water in alley behind St. Charles — week and counting
body_clean: Mayor, I know you hear from me often about this stretch and I apologize for that. The alley behind the 500 block of St. Charles has had standing water pooled near the garage entrances since last week's rain. My car has to drive through it every morning and the water splashes up into the wheel wells. It's also a mosquito concern as we get warmer. I've called Public Works twice and left messages. Would appreciate at minimum a work order number so I know someone has logged this.

subject: Standing water in alley behind St. Charles — week and counting
body_clean: Ms. Pawlak, work order WO-2025-0314 is open for the alley behind the 500 block of St. Charles. My crew inspected this morning — the alley drain grate was partially crushed, probably from garbage truck weight over the years, and the throat is restricted to about thirty percent of its design opening. We're ordering a replacement grate and frame; parts should arrive by end of next week. In the meantime we dug out the sediment to get whatever flow we can. I'm sorry your calls weren't returned — that's on us.

subject: Eastern Ave and 25th floods every time it rains
body_clean: Hello, I'm a homeowner at 2507 Eastern Ave. The intersection right in front of my house turns into a small lake after every decent rain, three springs running. The water gets deep enough that SUVs slow way down and sedans sometimes stall trying to push through. I've seen kids on bikes detour through my yard to avoid it. The storm drain on the southeast corner looks like it's there but I don't think it's actually doing anything. Can someone check whether it's connected or just sitting there?

subject: Eastern Ave and 25th floods every time it rains
body_clean: Mr. Tillman, thank you for the clear description. A non-functioning inlet at a busy intersection is a safety issue, not just an inconvenience. I'm forwarding your message to our Public Works Director Frank DiMeo today with a request for a camera inspection and a written assessment. You'll hear back with findings within two weeks. I appreciate your patience.

subject: Neighbor's sump pump discharge flowing onto my driveway — 1142 Granville Ave
body_clean: Good morning, I live at 1142 Granville Ave and my neighbor has a sump discharge pipe that runs across my driveway apron and empties into my front yard. During the April rains it ran almost continuously and eroded part of my lawn near the sidewalk. I'm not trying to get anyone in trouble but this doesn't feel right. Is there a village ordinance about where sump pumps can discharge? I'd like to handle this neighborly if possible but I need to know what the rules are first.

subject: Neighbor's sump pump discharge flowing onto my driveway — 1142 Granville Ave
body_clean: Ms. Orozco, Village Code Section 18-142 prohibits directing sump discharge onto adjacent private property or across public sidewalks in a manner that causes erosion or nuisance. A pipe crossing your driveway apron and draining into your lawn almost certainly qualifies. I'll schedule an inspection of 1140 Granville Ave this week. If the discharge is non-compliant we'll issue a courtesy notice first with a 15-day correction window before any formal action. I'll follow up with you after the inspection.

subject: Water coming in under my back door — 3319 Harvard Ave
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, after the rains on the 18th, water sheeted across my backyard and pooled against the foundation, then came in under the back door threshold. Maybe a half inch, but enough to warp my kitchen hardwood floor. I believe the ground behind my property on the alley side was regraded at some point and now slopes toward me instead of away. I'd like someone from the Village to take a look before the next storm.

subject: Water coming in under my back door — 3319 Harvard Ave
body_clean: Mr. Gibbs, I walked the alley behind Harvard Ave this morning with my crew chief. You're correct — the alley was resurfaced in 2021 and the crown elevation near your rear property line is higher than it should be relative to your yard. That's a grading error from that project and it's on us to fix. I'm going to have my guys hand-grade a swale along the alley edge behind your property and install a small French drain connection to the alley inlet at the 33rd Ave crossing. Rough grading should be done within two weeks if weather cooperates.

subject: Ponding at Bellwood Ave and 50th — school walking route at risk
body_clean: Hi Mayor, I walk my kids to school along Bellwood Ave every morning. The intersection at 50th has had a huge puddle spanning most of the crosswalk after every rain this spring. My kids have to walk in the street to get around it, which scares me with the traffic. The pond usually takes two or three days to drain. I don't know if there's a clogged drain or just a low spot, but for a school walking route this feels urgent.

subject: Ponding at Bellwood Ave and 50th — school walking route at risk
body_clean: Ms. Ferreira, Mayor Okonkwo asked me to let you know your concern has been logged and forwarded to Public Works as a priority given the school pedestrian route. Work Order WO-2025-0512 is open. The Director flagged it for inspection this week. Because you mentioned children walking in the street, he's also asked our traffic safety coordinator to look at temporary signage while the drainage issue is diagnosed. We'll follow up once we have findings.

subject: Sewer-like odor from basement floor drain since April rains — 408 Morris Ave
body_clean: Dear Mayor, I've been unable to reach anyone by phone at Village Hall this week. Since the heavy rains on April 9th our basement floor drain has been emitting a strong sewer-like odor. No water has come in but the smell is bad enough that we've closed the basement door and run fans. Our plumber checked our trap and says it's fine, so he believes the problem is in the main line or storm system. We are elderly and spend time down there for laundry. The smell is affecting our health. Please help.

subject: Sewer-like odor from basement floor drain since April rains — 408 Morris Ave
body_clean: Mr. Wierzbicki, I'm sorry you had difficulty reaching us and that this is affecting your health — that's unacceptable. I've personally contacted our Public Works Director and asked that your address be added to tomorrow morning's inspection schedule. Please keep the basement door closed and the fans running until we can investigate. If the odor worsens significantly before then, please call our after-hours line. Someone will follow up with you directly by end of week.

subject: Catch basin clogged on Rice Ave near 24th
body_clean: Hey Mayor Okonkwo, the catch basin on the north side of Rice Ave just west of 24th has been packed with leaves and branches since before winter. With all the spring rains it's overflowing onto the sidewalk and into the parkway. The water then runs down Rice toward the intersection and creates a big puddle. I walk past it every day on the way to the Metra station. Not an emergency but definitely needs a cleanout before it gets worse. Thanks for everything you do for the neighborhood.

subject: Flooding on Englewood Ave between 19th and 25th — getting worse
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I've lived on Englewood Ave twenty-two years and every spring there's a stretch between 19th and 25th that floods enough to make the road impassable for small cars. This year it's worse than usual. After the May 6th storm I measured roughly eight to ten inches of standing water. It took eighteen hours to subside. I understand there's a known low point there but I'm wondering whether the Village has any capital plans to address this or whether it's always just going to be a flood-and-wait situation. It's affecting my ability to get to work.

subject: Foundation seepage — 1703 Madison St — multiple walls this year
body_clean: Good afternoon Mayor, I'm writing on behalf of my family at 1703 Madison St. We've had minor basement seepage every spring for years but this April water came in through two spots along the foundation wall, not just the floor drain. We lost boxes of family documents and my husband's tools. Our neighbor at 1707 says the same thing is happening to her. We're wondering if the storm drain capacity on Madison is adequate or whether the block is dealing with a larger infrastructure problem. We have photographs if helpful.

subject: Drainage swale behind Frederick Ave completely overgrown
body_clean: Mayor, the open drainage swale behind the properties on the east side of Frederick Ave between Monroe and Madison has not been maintained in at least two years. It's choked with phragmites and volunteer brush. After last week's storms water backed up and I had standing water in my yard for three days. In past years the Village cleaned it in early spring. I'd like to know if there's a maintenance schedule that was skipped or if the responsibility has shifted somehow. I'm at 2215 Frederick Ave.

subject: Parkway drain near 917 Hirsch Ave — tree roots blocking it?
body_clean: Hi, I live at 917 Hirsch Ave and there's a section of parkway directly in front of my house that holds water for days after rain. The drain inlet nearby might be blocked by roots — the parkway tree next to it has huge surface roots going toward the grate. My kids play in the yard and I'm worried about mosquitoes breeding as the weather warms up. Could someone come take a look before summer?

subject: Construction site on 25th Ave has no erosion controls — mud running into my yard
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, there's a new construction project on 25th Ave just north of Washington and the site has no silt fence or erosion controls that I can see. Every time it rains, mud-laden water runs off the site and down the public sidewalk and into the adjacent yards, including mine at 2431 25th Ave. I have photos. I believe there are state and county requirements for erosion control on active construction sites. I'm asking the Village to inspect and require the contractor to install proper controls before the next storm.

subject: Seepage through window well at 1922 St. Paul Ave
body_clean: Hello, I'm a longtime resident at 1922 St. Paul Ave. This spring water has been seeping into my basement through the window well on the north side of the house. I had the well cleaned last fall so I don't think it's debris. After the May 12th storm the seepage was bad enough to require towels and a wet vac. I'm not sure if the street drainage or the parkway grading contributes, but the well fills faster than it can drain during moderate rain. Any guidance the Village can offer would be welcome.

subject: Sump pump running non-stop — overland flow from neighboring properties
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I've had my sump pump replaced twice in two years. Each time Public Works visits they say the pump is fine but the volume of water is too great. My yard is at the bottom of a gentle slope and the two properties above me on 46th Ave clearly drain toward me. During the March 27th rain my sump ran fourteen straight hours. I was up all night with a bucket. I'm not made of money and I can't keep replacing equipment to manage water that isn't coming from my property. Is there any Village program that addresses this kind of overland flow problem?

subject: Alley drain elevation problem behind 712 Hirsch Ave — survey data attached
body_clean: Mayor, I'm a retired civil engineer so I'll give you useful information rather than just a complaint. I've surveyed my backyard with a transit level and the alley behind 712 Hirsch drops toward me at approximately 1.2 percent. The alley drain nearest my rear lot line sits at least three inches higher than it should given the surrounding asphalt crown. In heavy rains my yard collects water with nowhere to go. I'm asking whether the Village can send a survey crew to verify what I'm seeing and confirm whether that drain was installed to correct elevation. Happy to share my notes.

subject: Thank you for fixing the Geneva Ave drain — finally worked this spring
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I just wanted to say thank you. I emailed your office last October about the clogged drain at the bottom of Geneva Ave near the viaduct and someone from Public Works came out and cleaned it. We had a big rain April 3rd and for the first time in years the street didn't flood. My kids walked to the bus stop without their boots on. Small thing maybe but it makes a real difference in daily life. Please thank whoever did the work. Your office has been much more responsive these past couple of years and that's noticed.
